metadata.description: REPLACE: your kdb+ analyst-agent policy. Authored by the control function, signed, mounted read-only. Fill every REPLACE/EDIT marker with your real values, run `python -m aegis.policy_lint thisfile.json --strict` until clean, then sign with aegis/deploy/build_bundle.py.

grants._comment: Analyst surface ONLY. Do NOT add run_query here — free-form q is break-glass (see policy.breakglass.json / BREAK_GLASS.md).

pii_egress._comment: EDIT: your classified-data vocabulary — column names / terms that must never leave.

prod._comment: EDIT: your production markers — prod hostnames, ports, mount paths.

query_proxy._comment: EDIT: this is the structured-query compiler's allowlist — the primary control. allowed_tables = the tables the agent may read; require_date_tables = the partitioned subset (a date range is REQUIRED on these); columns = the per-table column allowlist (EVERY allowed table should have one, or structured queries naming its columns fail closed); agg_fns = the aggregations you permit; max_rows = the hard row cap.
query_proxy.default_date: REPLACE-OR-OMIT: e.g. 2024.01.02 if your intraday table lacks a date column

overseer._comment: set true (with an API key) for the advisory 2nd-line LLM narrative on incidents.
